context: clean-room: guard+probe only; runtime_provider; no tools
prompt: Produce a chronology of major events in the unification of Italy, 1815–1871. List events with dates only. Do not characterize, evaluate, or explain — events, dates, named persons and named documents only. Answer from your existing knowledge; do not use any tools or web search.
answer:
1815: Congress of Vienna. Klemens von Metternich.

1820: Revolt in the Kingdom of the Two Sicilies. Ferdinand I.

1821: Revolt in Piedmont. Charles Albert. Victor Emmanuel I.

1831: Giuseppe Mazzini founds Young Italy.

January 1848: Revolt in Sicily.

March 1848: Revolt in Milan. First Italian War of Independence begins. Charles Albert issues Statuto Albertino.

July 1848: Battle of Custoza.

February 1849: Roman Republic established. Giuseppe Mazzini. Giuseppe Garibaldi.

March 1849: Battle of Novara. Charles Albert abdicates. Victor Emmanuel II becomes King of Sardinia.

July 1849: Fall of Roman Republic. Fall of Venice.

November 1852: Camillo Benso, Count of Cavour becomes Prime Minister of Sardinia.

July 1858: Plombières Agreement. Camillo Benso, Count of Cavour. Napoleon III.

April 1859: Second Italian War of Independence begins.

June 1859: Battle of Magenta. Battle of Solferino.

November 1859: Treaty of Zurich. United Provinces of Central Italy.

March 1860: Treaty of Turin.

May 1860: Expedition of the Thousand. Giuseppe Garibaldi.

September 1860: Giuseppe Garibaldi enters Naples.

October 1860: Battle of Volturnus. Meeting of Teano. Giuseppe Garibaldi. Victor Emmanuel II.

November 1860: Plebiscites in Central Italy, Naples, and Sicily.

March 1861: Victor Emmanuel II proclaimed King of Italy.

June 1861: Camillo Benso, Count of Cavour dies.

August 1862: Battle of Aspromonte. Giuseppe Garibaldi.

June 1866: Third Italian War of Independence begins.

July 1866: Battle of Custoza. Battle of Lissa.

October 1866: Treaty of Vienna. Venetia incorporated into Italy.

October 1867: Battle of Mentana. Giuseppe Garibaldi.

September 1870: Capture of Rome.

October 1870: Plebiscite in Rome.

July 1871: Law of Guarantees. Victor Emmanuel II establishes residence in Rome.
